test.name: Out-of-scope request — no skill should fire
test.description: User asks something entirely outside the genealogy toolkit's scope. No skill should activate. This tests the empty-correct_skill case: the model should recognize the request isn't within scope, decline politely, and not attempt to coerce the question into a genealogy skill.

input.user_message: Help me write a Python function that parses a CSV file

negative.explanation: This is a general programming request, not genealogy. None of the 23 genealogy skills should fire. Specifically: wiki-lookup should not interpret the request as 'look up CSV on Wikipedia' — that's a contorted reading that ignores the user's actual ask. The right response is to decline (or explain that programming help is outside the toolkit's scope), not to invoke any skill.

judge_context: Should respond briefly explaining the request is outside the genealogy toolkit's scope rather than coercing it into one of the genealogy skills
